codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit adds substantial Playwright coverage for the cross-workspace running-header scenario, but it does not fully satisfy the feature/skill contract because it omits the required source-document status-block updates and the promised backend-truth proof tied to the same seeded scenario.

issues.description: The feature's skill requires updating the mission status blocks for every directly used source doc, but commit 568e34d only changes `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts`. The cross-workspace tests rely on the timer-page family and top-composer behavior documented in `docs/research/toggl-timer-view-family.md` and `docs/research/toggl-timer-top-entry-form.md`, yet those docs still carry status blocks from a prior feature and were not updated for this feature. That means the implementation does not complete the required traceability work for the feature it claims to finish.

The fullstack worker procedure requires one shared seeded scenario spanning backend truth and browser-visible behavior, with failing tests on both sides as needed. The handoff claims the feature was already implemented and the commit adds only browser E2E tests; there is no backend regression or contract smoke added or updated in `apps/backend/internal/tracking/...` proving the same cross-workspace edit/stop/read-current scenario at the backend layer. As reviewed, the feature evidence falls short of the required backend-truth proof for a fullstack feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tracking-fullstack-worker` skill still requires source-doc status-block updates for every directly used doc, but this completed feature was accepted with only E2E test changes and no doc-block updates. Either enforcement is missing in the review flow or the skill should clarify when a proof-only feature may skip doc-block edits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 8 in `.factory/skills/tracking-fullstack-worker/SKILL.md` says to update status blocks for every directly used source doc. Commit `568e34d` changes only `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts`, while `docs/research/toggl-timer-view-family.md` and `docs/research/toggl-timer-top-entry-form.md` still show status blocks authored by `shared-timer-page-state-and-view-preference`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill promises a shared backend+browser proof path, but the worker transcript/handoff shows only browser tests were added for this feature because the implementation already existed. The skill may need to distinguish 'implementation feature' from 'coverage-only follow-up' work so reviewers have a clear expectation for whether backend-test additions are mandatory.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill steps 2, 4, 7, 9, and 11 all describe one shared seeded scenario with backend truth and browser proof. The handoff for feature `cross-workspace-running-header` states 'The frontend already implemented this behavior correctly' and lists only new cases in `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts`; no backend file appears in `git show --stat 568e34d`.

summary: Reviewed commit `568e34d` and the worker evidence for `cross-workspace-running-header`. The added Playwright coverage is relevant and appears aligned with the runtime behavior in `WorkspaceTimerPage`, but the feature does not satisfy the fullstack worker contract because it lacks the required source-doc traceability updates and any backend-truth proof tied to the same scenario, so this review fails.
